member.name: Yuan Yang
member.synopsis: Yuan Yang is the Labour MP for Earley and Woodley , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Energy: Prices
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps he is taking to help reduce household energy bills.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Tackling affordability is this government’s number one priority. The Government has acted on energy bills, with our decisions at last year’s Budget driving the price cap down: those decisions are now factored into bills for the years to come. We will cont...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Energy: Prices
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, whether his Department has made an estimate of the cost to the public purse of introducing a discounted essential energy block for all households; and how this compares to the current cost of
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the need to support vulnerable households which are struggling with bills whilst we transition to clean power. That is why the Autumn Budget acted to take an average £150 of costs off people’s energy bills. This support came on t...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.answerText: The consultation for the earned settlement model, as proposed in ‘A Fairer Pathway to Settlement’, was open to the public between 20 November 2025 and 12 February 2026. Contributions will now be analysed, and the findings will support the development of t...

writtenQuestions.heading: Immigration: Personal Income

writtenQuestions.heading: Visas: British National (Overseas)
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of raising the language requirement for settlement from B1 to B2 on BNO visa holders.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Being able to communicate effectively in English is essential for accessing public services, participating in civic life, securing employment, and building strong connections within local communities.The Government is continuing to consider whether the B2...

writtenQuestions.heading: Facility for Airborne Atmospheric Measurements

writtenQuestions.answerText: As part of a review of its infrastructure investments, the Natural Environment Research Council (NERC), within UK Research and Innovation (UKRI), has concluded that the Facility for Airborne Atmospheric Measurements (FAAM) no longer represents good value ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Statutory Sick Pay: Chronic Illnesses
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what discussions his Department has had with the Keep Britain Working team on the rate of statutory sick pay.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Sir Charlie Mayfield’s independent Keep Britain Working review report, published on 5 November 2025, aims to tackle health-related economic inactivity, with over one in five working-age adults out of the workforce, substantially because of health problems...

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS: Buildings
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether he has made an assessment of (a) how much new NHS primary estate is being built each year and (b) what the year-on-year change in that amount has been.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The primary care estate is a complex mix of general practice (GP) private ownership, third party ownership, and lease arrangements with approximately half of GP premises owned by GPs. As of December 2025, there are 6,210 GP surgeries across England.The Go...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: GP Practice Lists: Berkshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what the number of registered patients per GP is (a) across Earley and Woodley constituency, (b) the south Reading area and (c) West Berkshire.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As of September 2025, the number of registered patients per full time equivalent doctor in general practice was:2,698 in the Woodley and Earley constituency; and1,615 in West Berkshire.We cannot provide data for South Reading as it is not a formal geograp...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Visas: Skilled Workers
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what the current distribution of salaries by income tax bracket is for Skilled Worker visa holders in Earley and Woodley constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The information requested is not currently available from published statistics, and the relevant data could only be collated and verified for the purpose of answering this question at disproportionate cost.

writtenQuestions.heading: Neighbourhood Policing: Speed Limits
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what recent assessment she has made of the future viability of the Community Speedwatch Scheme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Decisions on adopting the Community Speedwatch scheme, and other schemes, are operational matters for individual Police and Crime Commissioners and Chief Constables to take, based on their local policing plans.They are best placed to understand how to mee...

writtenQuestions.heading: Special Educational Needs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what recent assessment she has made of the effectiveness of the system for supporting children with special educational needs and disabilities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There are challenges in the special educational needs and disabilities system. We will make mainstream education more inclusive through early identification of needs, effective support and high quality teaching while ensuring special schools can support t...

writtenQuestions.heading: Floods: Berkshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what assessment he has made of (a) trends in levels of (i) flooding and (ii) resulting closure of the B478 from Sonning to Playhatch and (b) the potential impact of climate change on f
writtenQuestions.answerText: The B478 between Sonning and Playhatch is located on the River Thames floodplain and is subject to frequent flooding. Sonning Quarry is located nearby where Tarmac Group Ltd is extracting minerals and undertaking restoration work. The Environment Agency i...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Magistrates: Voluntary Work
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, whether she plans to monitor the number of hours that magistrates volunteer each year.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Magistrates’ sitting levels are already monitored by HM Courts & Tribunals Service and the judiciary. Bench Chairs are responsible for ensuring magistrates meet their minimum sitting commitment of 13 days per year and for supporting effective listing ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Cancer: Disability
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the adequacy of access by disabled people to cancer screening; and what information his Department holds on such access.
writtenQuestions.answerText: NHS England is committed to improving the accessibility of the three cancer screening programmes which it commissions under the Public Health Services (S7a) agreement, particularly for under-served groups in society.Contractually, providers of National He...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

items.text: Role, work or services: Writing a book From: 11 July 2024. Payer: RCW Literary Agency (Literary agency), 20 Powis Mews, London W11 1JN (Registered 16 July 2024)

Payment: £31,753.40 Received on: 22 July 2025. Hours: Advance due one year after initial publication of the book 'Private Revolutions'. The book was completed before entering Parliament. Ultimate payer: Viking Penguin Inc, USA, 1745 Broadway, New York, NY 10019 (Registered 1 August 2025)

Payment: £1,081.87 Publication of book in Brazil, written before entering Parliament Received on: 19 May 2026. Hours: No additional hours. Ultimate payer: Todavia Libros, Rua Fidalga, 826 05432-000 São Paulo, Brazil (Registered 27 May 2026)

Role, work or services: Speaking engagement Until: 13 August 2024. Payer: Edinburgh International Book Festival (Literary festival), 121 George Street, Edinburgh EH2 4YN (Registered 18 September 2024)

Payment: £300 Spoke on two panels at the Edinburgh Book Festival. The first was a shared event with three authors; the second was an event which Yuan chaired. Received on: 9 September 2025. Hours: 2 hrs. (Registered 10 September 2025)

Role, work or services: Polish edition of book published From: 19 October 2024. Payer: Grupa Wydawnicza Foksal Ltd (Publishing company in Poland that have agreed to publish Yuan's book.), ul. Domaniewska 48, 02-672 Warszawa (Registered 21 November 2024)

Payment: £2,918.51 Received on: 21 October 2025. Hours: Payment received for book sales. Ultimate payer: RCW literary Agency (Literary Agency), RCW literary Agency, 20 Powis Mews, London W11 1JN (Registered 23 October 2025)
